overrides.override_summary: Maximum interest rate for late payments capped at 10% annually
overrides.why_it_matters: Limits the cost of late payment penalties; franchisor's standard 3% late charge plus potential interest cannot exceed 10% annualized

overrides.override_summary: Post-term covenant not to compete may not be enforceable under California law; no-poach/non-solicitation provision will not be enforced in California
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be free to open a competing business immediately after termination/expiration; also free to hire franchisor's employees

overrides.override_summary: New York choice of law provision may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law (which is generally more protective of franchisees) may apply instead of NY law

overrides.override_summary: New York forum selection clause may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to litigate in California rather than traveling to NY

overrides.override_summary: General release required at renewal/transfer subject to CA Corp Code §31512; does not waive CA Franchise Investment Law or Business & Professions Code §20010 rights
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees retain their statutory franchise law protections even after signing a general release

overrides.override_summary: Punitive/exemplary damages waiver not enforceable under CA Franchise Investment Law (Corp Code §31512)
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees can seek punitive damages in franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: No franchise questionnaire or acknowledgment can waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects franchisees from signing away their legal protections at closing

overrides.override_summary: Release language does not relieve franchisor from liability under Hawaii franchise laws
overrides.why_it_matters: HI franchise law protections survive general releases

overrides.override_summary: No franchise questionnaire or acknowledgment can waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance
overrides.why_it_matters: Protects HI franchisees from signing away legal protections

overrides.override_summary: Illinois law governs the Franchise Agreement (supersedes NY law)
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchise law (IFDA) applies, including its termination and non-renewal protections

overrides.override_summary: Any provision designating jurisdiction/venue outside Illinois is void (per IFDA §4)
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees can litigate in Illinois; cannot be forced to NY courts

overrides.override_summary: Rights upon termination and non-renewal governed by IFDA §§19-20
overrides.why_it_matters: IL statutory protections for franchisee termination/non-renewal apply

overrides.override_summary: IL Attorney General requires deferral of all initial franchise/development fees until franchisor satisfies pre-opening obligations and franchisee commences operations — imposed due to franchisor's financial condition
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisees do not pay upfront; fees only due after franchisor performs and business opens — significant protection given franchisor's weak financial condition

overrides.override_summary: Indiana law governs (supersedes NY law)
overrides.why_it_matters: Indiana franchise law protections apply

overrides.override_summary: Unilateral termination without good cause (material breach) prohibited per IC 23-2-2.7-1(7)
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees cannot be terminated without a material breach — stricter standard than FA defaults list

overrides.override_summary: Release language does not relieve franchisor from liability under Indiana franchise laws
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchise law protections survive general releases

overrides.override_summary: Jury trial waiver and punitive damages waiver both deleted for IN franchises
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees retain right to jury trial and can seek punitive damages

overrides.override_summary: Noncompete enforcement provisions (FA §12.04) do not apply in Indiana
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisees may have more flexibility in enforcement of noncompete provisions

overrides.override_summary: MD Securities Commissioner requires deferral of all initial fees until pre-opening obligations completed; ADA fees deferred until first franchise opens — due to franchisor's financial condition
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees protected from losing upfront investment if franchisor cannot perform

overrides.override_summary: General release does not apply to liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD statutory claims survive general releases at renewal/transfer

overrides.override_summary: MD franchise law may supersede FA on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: MD statutory protections apply to termination decisions

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for claims under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law (3-year statute of limitations)
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisees can bring franchise law claims in MD courts

overrides.override_summary: General release preserves GBL Article 33 rights (§687(4) and (5))
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law protections cannot be waived through general releases

overrides.override_summary: Choice of law does not waive GBL Article 33 rights
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchisees retain GBL protections regardless of choice-of-law provisions

overrides.override_summary: ND law governs (supersedes NY law)
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law protections apply, which are among the strongest in the nation

overrides.override_summary: All venue provisions designating NY courts deleted for ND franchises
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in NY

overrides.override_summary: General release requirement on renewal deleted entirely; release does not relieve from ND franchise law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees do not need to sign any general release to renew their franchise

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompetes generally considered unenforceable per NDCC §9-08-06
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees are essentially free to compete immediately after franchise ends

overrides.override_summary: Both jury trial waiver and punitive/exemplary damages waiver deleted for ND
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees retain full jury trial and punitive damages rights

overrides.override_summary: ND requires deferral of initial fees until all franchisor obligations fulfilled and franchisee commences business — due to financial capability concerns
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees do not risk upfront investment if franchisor cannot deliver on pre-opening promises

overrides.override_summary: WI Fair Dealership Act (Ch. 135) governs; requires 90-day notice for termination/cancellation/non-renewal/substantial change and 60-day cure period
overrides.why_it_matters: WI franchisees get substantially longer notice (90 vs. 30 days) and cure periods (60 vs. 30 days) than the FA provides

overrides.override_summary: No franchise questionnaire or acknowledgment can waive franchise law claims or disclaim reliance
overrides.why_it_matters: WI franchisees protected from signing away legal protections
